7992	  affine morphism: separatedness makes the immersion \(j\) quasi-separated and the
7993	  preimage of any affine open of \(X\) meets \(U\) in an affine, so \(j\) is affine.
7994	  The relative affine vanishing for affine morphisms then gives \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} =
7995	  0\) for \(q \geq 1\); concretely, by the presheaf description
7996	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}) \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H}\) is the

8001	  (2) Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{H} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
8002	  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-modules and apply \(j_*\) degreewise. Since each \(\mathcal{I}^n\)
8003	  is \(j_*\)-acyclic by part (1) (\(R^q j_*\mathcal{I}^n = 0\) for \(q \geq 1\)), the
8004	  complex \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is a resolution of \(j_*\mathcal{H}\).
8005

8007	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}), \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n)\) is the
8008	  sheafification of

8011	      \;=\; H^k\bigl(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
8012	  \]

8014	  \(\mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\) along the open immersion
8015	  \(U \cap f^{-1}(V) \hookrightarrow f^{-1}(V)\), which leaves cohomology unchanged. For

8017	  \(X\) being separated and \(j\) affine, the affine open \(U\) meets it in the affine
8018	  open \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\); hence by Serre vanishing
8019	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} the cohomology
8020	  \(H^k(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\).

8038	  \emph{Formalization route.} The acyclicity argument above rests on three identifications,
8039	  which a formalization realizes as follows.
8040	
8041	  \emph{Bridge (1): the cohomology-presheaf identification.} The objectwise homology in degree
8042	  \(n\) of the pushed-forward resolution complex \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\), evaluated at an open
8043	  \(V\), is identified with the absolute cohomology presheaf
8044	  \(V \mapsto H^n(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}) = \operatorname{Ext}^n\bigl(j_!\mathcal{O}_{f^{-1}(V)},
8045	  \mathcal{G}\bigr)\). Concretely: \(\operatorname{Ext}^n(X, Y)\) over an injective resolution of
8046	  the second argument \(Y\) is identified with the \(n\)-th cohomology of the \(\operatorname{Hom}\)
8047	  cochain complex \(H^n\bigl(\operatorname{Hom}(X[0], \mathcal{I}^\bullet)\bigr)\), via the composite
8048	  of the Ext-to-cohomology-class equivalence and the cohomology-class-to-homology equivalence of the
8049	  Hom complex (the equivalences named in Bridge anchor
8050	  Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_homcomplex_mathlib}). Taking \(X = j_!\mathcal{O}_{f^{-1}(V)}\) and \(Y =
8051	  \mathcal{G}\), the degree-\(k\) Hom group \(\operatorname{Hom}(j_!\mathcal{O}_{f^{-1}(V)},
8052	  \mathcal{I}^k)\) is identified with \(\Gamma(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{I}^k) = (f_*\mathcal{I}^k)(V)\) by
8053	  the corepresentability \(j_!\mathcal{O}_U \dashv \Gamma(U, -)\) of the absolute-cohomology setup,
8054	  degreewise and naturally; the \(\mathbb{N}\)/\(\mathbb{Z}\) indexing of the two cochain complexes is
8055	  matched by the resolution's degreewise comparison isomorphism. The right-derived side is settled by
8056	  the already-used identification of \(R^n f_*\) with the homology of \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\)
8057	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}); no separate ``right-derived equals Ext'' comparison
8058	  lemma is needed, since both compute the same homology over the same injective resolution and meet at
8059	  corepresentability.
8060	
8061	  \emph{Bridge (2): Serre-vanishing transport to a general affine open.} The Serre vanishing of
8062	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} is stated on \(\operatorname{Spec} R\). For a general affine
8063	  open \(V\), the preimage \(j^{-1}(V)\) (respectively \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\)) is again affine: \(j\) is
8064	  an affine morphism (the helper that \(U \hookrightarrow X\) is affine for \(U\) affine and \(X\)
8065	  separated), so preimages of affines under \(j\) are affine, and one transports the vanishing along
8066	  the canonical isomorphism \(j^{-1}(V) \cong \operatorname{Spec}\Gamma(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{O})\) using
8067	  the naturality of absolute cohomology under that isomorphism.

8069	  \emph{Bridge (3): locally-zero \(\Rightarrow\) zero sheafification, for module sheafification.} The
8070	  passage from ``\(H^k(\cdots) = 0\) on a basis of affine opens'' to ``\(R^k f_* = 0\)'' is the

8073	  abelian-sheaf statement across the natural isomorphism

8075	  \operatorname{forget}\) between module-sheafification followed by the forgetful functor and abelian
8076	  sheafification; the ``locally zero'' hypothesis it consumes is exactly the basis-of-affines vanishing
8077	  supplied by Bridges (1) and~(2).

8098	  (additive). A composite of additive functors is additive; the additivity instance is

8106	[Sheafification of a sectionwise-locally-zero presheaf vanishes]
8107	  \label{lem:isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_sections_locally_zero}

8109	  \uses{lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero}
8110	  Let \(J\) be a Grothendieck topology on a site \(\mathcal{C}\) and \(Q\) a presheaf of
8111	  abelian groups. Suppose that for every object \(U\) and every section \(s \in Q(U)\) there
8112	  is a covering sieve \(S \in J(U)\) on which \(s\) restricts to \(0\) --- i.e.\ \(Q(g)(s) =
8113	  0\) for every arrow \(g : V \to U\) in \(S\). Then the sheafification of \(Q\) is the zero
8114	  sheaf.

8116	  This is the \emph{sectionwise} (local-injectivity) strengthening of
8117	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero}. That lemma requires the
8118	  \emph{objectwise} vanishing \(Q(V) = 0\) for every member \(V\) of a single covering sieve,
8119	  which forces the vanishing to hold on a downward-closed family. The present hypothesis only
8120	  asks each individual section to die on \emph{some} covering sieve, so the witnessing sieve
8121	  may be generated by a cofinal \emph{basis} of good opens (such as the affine opens, where
8122	  the relevant section group already vanishes) even though that basis is not downward closed
8123	  --- exactly the situation of the relative-affine vanishing argument, where the affine opens
8124	  are not downward closed in the opens topology.

8130	  local surjectivity is automatic because the target is objectwise a zero object. Hence \(0 :
8131	  Q \to Z\) is locally bijective, i.e.\ lies in the local-isomorphism class \(J.W\), and
